summary: I read both guides and the full work under review — the three `Dashboard::` query objects, `Dashboard::Broadcast`, the `HomeController`/`PhasesController`, the wired-in broadcast call sites (Complete, Sweep, ManagerTick, Approve, Finalize, ReworkToPhase, BroadcastCard/Column), the entire `home/` view tree, the Stimulus controllers, the `status_badge` helper, and the guide additions made in this PR — then cross-checked the sibling critics' verdicts for severity calibration. The code under review is byte-identical to iteration 4 (only `input.json` changed), so I independently re-verified each prior finding against the current tree rather than assuming. The architecture is strongly guide-aligned: query-object POROs, thin controllers that branch only on the Result, service-layer post-commit broadcasts targeting the smallest DOM unit over a per-user stream with progress ticks opting out, the shared semantic `status_badge`, a properly scoped presentation-boundary rescue, and both genuinely new patterns documented in the guides in the same PR per CLAUDE.md. No blockers or majors surfaced; six real but minor, guide-cited deviations (red-500/green-500 status fills, a hand-rolled attention pill duplicating `status_badge`, run-selection logic living in a view helper, empty states missing icon+action, the poll-based fleet panel without a guide amendment, and off-scale spacing values) remain unaddressed, so I wrote `verdict.json` as **needs_work** with those six minor findings and a fresh iteration-5 summary.
